close_reason: Fixed: validate_key_type now checks ECC curve (NistP256) and RSA key size (Rsa2048); also called from sign()
description: validate_key_type() (line 528) matches only the Public enum discriminant (Ecc vs Rsa) but does not verify the actual curve or key size. A P-384 ECC key passes as ecdsa-p256, and a 4096-bit RSA key passes as rsa-pss-2048. Consequences: (1) public_key_der() returns an SPKI whose algorithm OID contradicts the SigningAlgorithm claim; (2) normalize_signature() silently truncates oversized ECC scalars (48-byte r/s for P-384 → 32 bytes via saturating_sub), producing invalid signatures. Additionally, validate_key_type is called in public_key_der() but not in sign(), so the sign path has no early guard at all — it relies on normalize_signature's match arm, which matches EcDsa regardless of curve. Fix: destructure the Public::Ecc { parameters, .. } variant and assert parameters.ecc_curve() == EccCurve::NistP256; destructure Public::Rsa { parameters, .. } and assert parameters.key_bits() == RsaKeyBits::Rsa2048. Also call validate_key_type at the top of the sign() spawn_blocking closure.
title: validate_key_type skips curve and key-size checks
